output: Executed batch 2 task T2. Added server-side `ResolvedSelectionContext` resolution from the live timeline, updated `create_task` to use resolved selection entries for lineage lookup, verified gallery selections never resolve as timeline anchors, and wrote the batch checkpoint.
deviations: Added one supporting plumbing change outside the listed file set: `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts` now passes the live session timeline id into `executeCreateTask()` so server-side selection resolution can stamp the correct `timeline_id`.

Updated two adjacent edge test files outside the listed file set to verify the new helper and the create-task integration path: `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/selectedClips.test.ts` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.test.ts`.
Ran the edge suites with `--config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts` because the default app Vitest config excludes `supabase/functions/**`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `ResolvedSelectionContext`, resolved it from the live timeline config on the edge, switched `create-task` to derive lineage from the resolved selection entries, and verified the resolver and create-task integration via the updated edge test files.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified in code and tests that `resolveSelectionContext()` rejects `gallery-` clip ids as placement anchors and separately requires the clip id to exist in the live timeline config before setting `is_on_timeline=true`; gallery-prefixed and missing clip ids both resolve to `source='gallery'` with `is_on_timeline=false`.
